frames.name: Strategic Incoherence Frame
frames.description: Portrays Trump's Iran war as strategically confused, with shifting objectives and contradictory messaging revealing absence of clear planning
frames.emphasis: Trump's contradictory statements, lack of clear strategy, and hour-by-hour spinning as evidence of poor decision-making

frames.name: Campaign Promise Betrayal Frame
frames.description: Frames the war as Trump abandoning his 'America First' anti-war promises and betraying his base who voted for peace
frames.emphasis: Trump's hypocrisy in becoming the 'warmongering neocon' he once criticized, disappointing supporters like Tucker Carlson

frames.name: Messaging Chaos Frame
frames.description: Focuses on the administration's scattered communication strategy through multiple interviews rather than formal addresses
frames.emphasis: Trump's scattershot media approach and varying timelines/objectives depending on the outlet and timing

frames.name: Risk-Benefit Analysis Frame
frames.description: Presents balanced assessment of potential strategic gains versus dangers of military action against Iran
frames.emphasis: Weighing degraded Iranian capabilities and deterrence against risks of retaliation and regional instability

frames.name: Domestic Political Calculation Frame
frames.description: Views the war through lens of Trump's domestic political needs, particularly regarding midterm elections
frames.emphasis: War as serving Trump's need to assert emergency authority and avoid electoral 'regime change' at home

outlierPerspectives: The Guardian's framing of the war as primarily about Trump's narcissism and domestic political survival rather than foreign policy objectives
Foreign Policy's emphasis on Trump betraying his peace-candidate image and disappointing his base
The Atlantic's focus on the tactical-before-strategic approach as fundamentally backwards military planning
missingContext: Congressional oversight and authorization questions - most sources mention Trump 'shut out congressional consultation' but don't explore constitutional implications
Allied reactions and international law considerations - limited coverage of how key allies view the legality and wisdom of the strikes
Iranian domestic political dynamics - little analysis of how the strikes affect Iranian internal politics beyond regime change speculation
Economic and energy market impacts - minimal coverage of oil prices, sanctions, or broader economic consequences
Regional proxy conflicts - limited exploration of how the war affects ongoing conflicts in Gaza, Lebanon, Yemen where Iranian proxies operate
